question: Will the Republicans win the Delaware Senate race in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 midterm Delaware U.S. Senate election, inclusive of any run-offs.

A candidate shall be considered to represent a party in the event that he or she is the nominee of the party in question. Candidates other than the Democratic or Republican nominee (e.g., Greens, Libertarian, independent) may be added at a later date.

Candidates who run as independents will not be encompassed by the “Democrat” or “Republican” options regardless of any affiliation they may have with the party.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on the official certification.

eventMetadata.context_description: Lisa Blunt Rochester's commanding lead in recent polls, averaging 20-30 points over Republican Eric Ram, anchors trader consensus at 93.5% for a Democratic hold in Delaware's Senate race. The state's deep blue tilt—evident in Biden's 19-point 2020 win and unbroken Democratic Senate control since 1973—bolsters this pricing, alongside Blunt Rochester's fundraising dominance and congressional experience. GOP historical base rates show no competitive statewide wins in decades. Realistic challengers include a major Democratic scandal, unexpected national Republican surge, or late polling shift, though none have materialized amid stable voter registration edges for Democrats; watch October debates for volatility.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff's incumbency advantage anchors trader consensus at 81.5% for a Democratic hold in Georgia's 2026 Senate race, bolstered by his strong fundraising and early polling leads over potential Republican challengers like state Rep. Mike Collins or Courtney Kramer. Georgia's battleground dynamics, where Democrats have won recent Senate contests despite narrow Republican presidential victories—including Trump's 2024 Georgia win—support this tilt, though GOP recruitment efforts could narrow the gap. Recent developments, such as Ossoff's legislative visibility on bipartisan bills and the absence of a consensus GOP nominee ahead of the May 2026 primary, sustain high Democratic implied probabilities amid uncertainty over voter turnout and national headwinds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Jim Risch's dominant position drives the 91.5% trader consensus for a GOP win in Idaho's Senate race, bolstered by the state's strong Republican lean—Trump carried it by 31 points in 2020—and Risch's long tenure chairing the Foreign Relations Committee. Recent May 21 primaries saw Risch easily dispatch minor challengers, while Democrat Kathryn Davidson advanced unopposed amid weak party infrastructure; polling averages show Risch leading 55-25. Historical base rates for red-state incumbents exceed 95% re-election, aligning with current implied probabilities. Realistic challenges include a major Risch scandal, health issues at age 81, or an improbable national Democratic wave, though none appear on horizon ahead of November 5.

Illinois' strong Democratic tilt, with President Biden's 17-point 2020 win and a D+7 partisan voter index, bolsters this outlook amid Durbin's long tenure and fundraising dominance post-March primaries. Trader sentiment reflects historical base rates for safe blue seats holding firm even in competitive cycles. Realistic challenges include a national Republican wave, Durbin health issues, or turnout collapse among urban Democrats, though current evidence shows minimal momentum shifts ahead of November.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Joni Ernst's consistent double-digit polling lead over Democrat Rick Sorensen, alongside Iowa's strong Republican lean where Donald Trump holds a 10-point advantage in recent surveys, anchors trader consensus at 62.5% for a GOP win in the Senate race. Recent developments include mid-October polls from Emerson and Trafalgar reaffirming her 8-11 point edge, robust Republican early voting turnout surpassing 2020 levels, and minimal Democratic momentum despite national Senate battleground focus elsewhere, sustaining these implied probabilities amid stable fundamentals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Sen. Jerry Moran's commanding lead in deep-red Kansas underpins the 82.5% trader consensus for a Republican Senate win, reflecting the state's consistent GOP dominance and historical base rates for incumbent holds in R+13 districts. Recent Emerson and Fabrizio polls confirm Moran ahead of Democrat Scott Fawcett by 25-26 points, with no major shifts post-GOP primary where Moran cruised unopposed. Strong fundraising—Moran at $2.5M cash-on-hand versus Fawcett's under $100K—and absent controversies sustain these odds, while low Democratic turnout in safe seats bolsters the implied probability amid steady early voting trends.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Republicans at 91% implied probability for the Kentucky Senate winner, driven by the state's entrenched GOP dominance—Trump carried it by 26 points in 2020, and retiring Sen. Mitch McConnell won by similar margins historically. GOP nominee Aaron A. Reed, who clinched the May primary with 35% plurality over Daniel Cameron, holds double-digit leads in all major polls, including recent Emerson and AtlasIntel surveys showing 17-20 point advantages over Democrat Allison Casey Gruber amid superior fundraising and Trump endorsement momentum. Realistic challenges include a major scandal hitting Reed, depressed GOP turnout, or a national Democratic wave, though current evidence points to low upset risk ahead of the November ballot.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Sen. Angus King's double-digit polling leads drive trader consensus at 73.5% for the Democratic-aligned outcome in Maine's Senate race. Absent late surprises before the November 5 election, markets reflect steady favoritism for King retaining the seat.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amy Klobuchar's substantial lead in recent Minnesota Senate polls, consistently above 20 points over Republican challenger Royce White, anchors trader consensus at 86.5% for a Democratic victory. Surveys from Star Tribune, Trafalgar Group, and UMass Lowell through late October confirm her dominance, bolstered by incumbency, strong fundraising, and Minnesota's Democratic tilt in federal races. White's campaign struggles, including primary infighting, missed filings, and polarizing statements on debt and foreign policy, have eroded Republican chances, with no poll narrowing the gap. Early voting trends favor Democrats, and forecasters rate the seat safe blue, though final turnout remains a wildcard ahead of November 5.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus gives Republicans an 83.5% implied probability to win Montana's Senate seat, driven by challenger Tim Sheehy's consistent polling lead over incumbent Democrat Jon Tester in a state Donald Trump carried by 16 points in 2020. Recent statewide surveys, including Emerson (Oct. 23-24) and AtlasIntel (Oct. 21-27), show Sheehy ahead 4-5 points amid national GOP momentum on economy and border issues. The October 25 debate highlighted Sheehy's military background and outsider appeal, boosting his position despite Tester's incumbency and fundraising advantages. With Election Day on November 5, rural turnout remains a key variable for final probabilities.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democratic incumbent Jeanne Shaheen's double-digit leads in recent polls drive the 89% trader consensus for a Democratic win in the New Hampshire Senate race, reflecting her incumbency advantage and the state's modest Democratic tilt. Latest surveys from UNH and Suffolk/USA Today show Shaheen ahead 51%-37% over Republican nominee Tejasinha Sivalingam, who won a fragmented GOP primary on September 10 with just 27% amid low turnout and internal divisions. Shaheen's superior fundraising and consistent polling post-primary have widened her edge, with traders pricing in limited GOP pickup potential despite national headwinds for Democrats. An October 15 debate looms as the next catalyst, though historical precedents favor strong incumbents here.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democratic Rep. eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat Jack Reed holds a commanding position in Rhode Island's Senate race, reflecting the state's deep-blue partisan lean where registered Democrats outnumber Republicans 4-to-1 and no GOP candidate has won statewide since 1994. Reed's 27-year tenure, consistent 65-70% victory margins, and scant opposition funding underscore trader consensus at 92.5% for a Democratic hold. Recent GOP primary victory by underfunded challenger Patrick Culhane produced no polling shift or momentum. Realistic challenges include a national Republican wave overwhelming local dynamics or an unforeseen Reed scandal, though Rhode Island's historical base rates favor stability ahead of November voting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Sen. Mike Rounds holds a commanding lead in the South Dakota Senate race, reflecting the state's deep-red partisan makeup where Donald Trump won by 26 points in 2020 and Republicans dominate statewide offices. Recent polls, including a September South Dakota News Watch survey showing Rounds ahead 54%-32% against Democrat Brian Bengs, underscore this edge, bolstered by Rounds' strong fundraising—over $3 million raised—and lack of credible Democratic infrastructure in the state. Trader consensus at 92% for the Republican nominee prices in minimal upset risk, though realistic challenges could emerge from an unforeseen Rounds scandal, health issue, or anomalous rural voter turnout shift favoring Democrats. Upcoming early voting and debates through November may introduce volatility.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Republican Jim Justice at 93% implied probability to win West Virginia's Senate seat, reflecting the state's deep-red political landscape where Donald Trump carried over 68% in 2020 and Joe Biden just 26%. Justice, the term-limited incumbent governor with high approval ratings since switching parties in 2017, maintains double-digit leads in recent polls like the October WVU survey (Justice 54%, Elliott 38%) and post-debate analyses showing no momentum shift for Democrat Glenn Elliott. His Trump endorsement and fundraising edge solidify dominance, though scenarios like a major Justice scandal or unforeseen Elliott ground-game surge could narrow the gap amid West Virginia's reliable GOP voter base.

Trader consensus at 92% for Republican odds captures this dominance, aligned with Wyoming's R+25 partisan lean and Trump carryover from 2020. Realistic challenges include a major scandal or national Democratic surge, though base rates suggest minimal risk ahead of November balloting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Sen. Roger Wicker's commanding win in the March 12 GOP primary, securing over 50% against multiple challengers, has solidified his path to the November 5 general election against Democratic nominee Robert G. Stovall, fueling trader consensus at 89.5% for a Republican victory in the solidly red Mississippi Senate race. Recent polls from Fabrizio Lee & Associates (April 2024) show Wicker leading 58%-32%, reflecting the state's strong GOP lean—Trump carried it by 16 points in 2020—and incumbents' historical 90%+ re-election rates in such environments. No major shifts from fundraising edges or endorsements have emerged, with low Democratic turnout in primaries underscoring limited competitiveness; traders price in minimal upset risk absent national wave dynamics.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Sen. James Lankford's commanding lead in Oklahoma's deep-red Senate race anchors trader consensus at 94.5% for a GOP win, reflecting consistent double-digit polling advantages over Democrat Chris Long. Lankford overcame early 2024 primary challenges tied to his bipartisan border security bill, securing 64% in the June runoff amid strong conservative turnout in a state Donald Trump carried by 33 points in 2020. Recent surveys from SoonerPoll and others show his support steady above 55%, bolstered by incumbency and limited Democratic infrastructure. Realistic challenges remain slim—absent a major scandal, health issue, or improbable national blue wave—but traders price in Oklahoma's historical Republican dominance for Class III seats.

A candidate shall be considered to represent a party in the event that he or she is the nominee of the party in question. Candidates other than the Democratic or Republican nominee (e.g., Greens, Libertarian, independent) may be added at a later date.

Candidates who run as independents will not be encompassed by the “Democrat” or “Republican” options regardless of any affiliation they may have with the party.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on the official certification.

events.title: Ohio Senate Election Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Ohio U.S. Senate special election currently scheduled for November 3, inclusive of any run-offs.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns Democrats a narrow 51.5% implied probability over Republicans at 48% in the Ohio Senate race, closely tracking polling aggregates that show incumbent Sherrod Brown and challenger Bernie Moreno tied within the margin of error. Ohio's status as a battleground state—red-shifted since 2020 yet bolstered by Brown's incumbency advantage and voter-approved abortion protections—fuels the deadlock amid intense ad blitzes and Trump's Moreno endorsement. Recent polls reflect steady parity despite economic headwinds favoring Republicans. Dynamics could shift via final debate outcomes, early voting data, turnout models, or national coattails on Election Day.

question: Will Google have the top AI model at the end of June 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model with the highest arena score based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Arena Score" section on the Leaderboard tab of https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text set to default (style control on) will be used to resolve this market.

If two models are tied for the best arena score at this market's check time, resolution will be based on whichever company's name, as it is described in this market group, comes first in alphabetical order (e.g., if both were tied, "Google" would resolve to "Yes", and "xAI" would resolve to "No")

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolves based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anthropic commands a 53.5% implied probability as the company with the top AI model by June's end, propelled by Claude 3.5 Sonnet's June 20 release, which vaulted to the summit of the LMSYS Chatbot Arena leaderboard with a leading Elo score of 1285—surpassing OpenAI's GPT-4o (1281) and Google's Gemini 1.5 Pro (1278). Traders cite its superior performance in coding, math, and agentic tasks per verified benchmarks like GPQA and SWE-bench, with no confirmed counter-releases from rivals by month's close. Google's 22.5% odds reflect hopes for a Gemini update, while OpenAI's 11% trails amid delays on advanced reasoning models like o1 expansions; xAI and DeepSeek linger lower absent major June launches. Market consensus hinges on leaderboard snapshots, underscoring benchmark volatility.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anthropic commands a 53.5% implied probability as the company with the top AI model by June's end, propelled by Claude 3.5 Sonnet's June 20 release, which vaulted to the summit of the LMSYS Chatbot Arena leaderboard with a leading Elo score of 1285—surpassing OpenAI's GPT-4o (1281) and Google's Gemini 1.5 Pro (1278). Traders cite its superior performance in coding, math, and agentic tasks per verified benchmarks like GPQA and SWE-bench, with no confirmed counter-releases from rivals by month's close. Google's 22.5% odds reflect hopes for a Gemini update, while OpenAI's 11% trails amid delays on advanced reasoning models like o1 expansions; xAI and DeepSeek linger lower absent major June launches. Market consensus hinges on leaderboard snapshots, underscoring benchmark volatility.

events.title: Largest Company end of June?

eventMetadata.context_description: NVIDIA's market-implied 82.5% probability of being the world's largest company by market cap at end of June stems primarily from its AI chip dominance, with shares surging 150% YTD amid insatiable demand for Hopper and upcoming Blackwell GPUs. Recent catalysts include a 10-for-1 stock split on June 7 that broadened retail access and propelled the stock past $3.3 trillion valuation, briefly overtaking Microsoft and Apple. Apple's 7.6% odds reflect WWDC's Apple Intelligence delays to 2025, tempering enthusiasm despite iPhone integration hype, while Alphabet's 7.5% trails on Gemini AI progress but lags NVIDIA's GPU moat. Saudi Aramco's 1.1% fades amid stable oil prices, with Microsoft, Amazon, and Tesla under 1% on execution risks. Traders eye final trading days for volatility.

Apple's 7.6% odds reflect WWDC's Apple Intelligence delays to 2025, tempering enthusiasm despite iPhone integration hype, while Alphabet's 7.5% trails on Gemini AI progress but lags NVIDIA's GPU moat. Saudi Aramco's 1.1% fades amid stable oil prices, with Microsoft, Amazon, and Tesla under 1% on execution risks. Traders eye final trading days for volatility.

question: Will the Democrats win the Louisiana Senate race in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 midterm Louisiana U.S. Senate election, inclusive of any run-offs.

A candidate shall be considered to represent a party in the event that he or she is the nominee of the party in question. Candidates other than the Democratic or Republican nominee (e.g., Greens, Libertarian, independent) may be added at a later date.

Candidates who run as independents will not be encompassed by the “Democrat” or “Republican” options regardless of any affiliation they may have with the party.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on the official certification.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Sen. Bill Cassidy's dominant position in Louisiana's U.S. Senate race drives the 90.5% trader consensus for a GOP victory, fueled by his consistent double-digit leads in recent polls averaging 57% support versus Democrats' 18-20%. Louisiana's deep-red electorate, where Republicans hold supermajorities and Trump won by 25 points in 2020, bolsters this edge amid Cassidy's strong fundraising and Trump endorsement. The November 5 all-party primary, with top two advancing to a potential December 7 runoff, favors Cassidy avoiding a competitive matchup. Realistic challenges include a GOP vote split elevating a Democrat to second place or an unforeseen scandal eroding his incumbency advantage, though current evidence suggests low probability.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pete Ricketts' decisive Republican primary win in May has solidified his frontrunner status in the Nebraska Senate race, fueling trader consensus at 74% implied probability for a GOP victory amid the state's strong Republican lean (R+13 Cook PVI). Recent polls, including Emerson's June survey showing Ricketts leading Democrat Tony Vargas 54%-36%, reinforce this edge, with no major shifts from fundraising dominance or Vargas' modest gains. Nebraska's history of lopsided Senate results—GOP margins over 20 points in recent cycles—further bolsters the outlook, while low Democratic odds at 4% reflect limited base mobilization potential ahead of November balloting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus slightly favors Rep. Mary Peltola at 51.5% over incumbent Sen. Dan Sullivan's 48.5% in the Alaska Senate race, reflecting intense competition under the state's ranked-choice voting system that amplifies independent and crossover voter influence. Sullivan benefits from strong Republican fundraising—over $10 million raised—and national GOP tailwinds, while Peltola capitalizes on her House incumbency popularity from the 2022 upset and appeal to moderates. Recent drivers include stagnant polls showing single-digit leads, mutual attack ads on energy policy and inflation, and thin turnout projections; separation could emerge from late October debates, Trump-endorsed rallies, or absentee ballot data before the November 5 contest.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the Alaska gubernatorial race favors Democrat Tom Begich at 25%, but remains tightly clustered among challengers Bernadette Wilson, Edna DeVries, and Treg Taylor due to a fragmented field ahead of the 2026 top-four primary and ranked-choice general election. Incumbent Republican Gov. Mike Dunleavy has not confirmed re-election plans, leaving room for Lt. Gov. Nancy Dahlstrom and Anchorage Mayor David Bronson to vie for GOP support without a unifying figure. Recent candidate announcements and early internal polling show no dominant lead, with independents and cross-party appeals complicating consolidation. Separation could emerge from Dunleavy's decision, major endorsements like Sen. Lisa Murkowski's, or first public surveys expected soon.

question: MegaETH market cap (FDV) >$4B one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of MegaETH's token is greater than $4,000,000,000 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 24 hours after launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If MegaETH doesn't launch a token by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No."

question: Will Israel annex Gaza territory by June 30 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel officially annexes any territory in the Gaza Strip by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Annexation is defined as an official declaration or legal act by the Israeli government claiming sovereignty over territory in the Gaza Strip that they were not claiming at the time of this market's creation.

Qualifying examples of annexation include the 1980 Jerusalem Law, and the Golan Heights Law. Instances where Israeli settlers claim administrative control over land in the Gaza Strip without formal annexation will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Israeli government. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will Israel annex Gaza territory by June 30 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel officially annexes any territory in the Gaza Strip by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Annexation is defined as an official declaration or legal act by the Israeli government claiming sovereignty over territory in the Gaza Strip that they were not claiming at the time of this market's creation.

Qualifying examples of annexation include the 1980 Jerusalem Law, and the Golan Heights Law. Instances where Israeli settlers claim administrative control over land in the Gaza Strip without formal annexation will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Israeli government. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Israel's lack of any official annexation plans for Gaza territory drives the strong trader consensus favoring "No" at 93.5% by June 30, 2026, as military operations remain focused on dismantling Hamas infrastructure rather than establishing sovereignty. Recent developments, including expanded buffer zones in northern Gaza and Philadelphi Corridor operations announced in late 2024, emphasize temporary security measures without Knesset legislation or cabinet approval for permanent control. International opposition from the U.S., EU, and Arab states, coupled with stalled ceasefire talks and domestic Israeli debates prioritizing hostage recovery over territorial expansion, reinforces low expectations. Upcoming U.S. policy shifts post-January 2025 inauguration add uncertainty but have not shifted odds meaningfully amid no primary source indications of annexation intent.

question: Will Donald Trump win the Nobel Peace Prize in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, as announced by the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

If multiple listed individuals or entities jointly receive the prize, the market will resolve to a single winner. If Donald Trump, Volodymyr Zelenskyy,  Benjamin Netanyahu, Vladimir Putin, or Elon Musk are among the recipients, the market will resolve to the highest-ranked among them in that exact order of precedence. If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. If no prioritized individuals are among the winners and all listed recipients are of the same type (all individuals or all organizations), the market will resolve to the person whose last name, or the entity’s name, comes first in alphabetical order as listed in this market.

If no official announcement regarding the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize has been made by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other."

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement from the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize tilts toward Volodymyr Zelenskyy at 10.3% implied probability, fueled by his unyielding resistance to Russian aggression amid stalled peace talks, positioning him as a symbol of democratic defiance. Yulia Navalnaya trails closely at 8.5%, her rising profile as heir to Alexei Navalny's anti-authoritarian legacy amplifying calls from human rights advocates, while Donald Trump's 7.5% reflects optimism around potential U.S.-brokered Middle East deals post-election. With top contenders neck-and-neck, dynamics hinge on the Norwegian Nobel Committee's bias toward grassroots activists over politicians—evident in recent awards to nuclear survivors—versus breakthrough diplomacy; resolution criteria demand official October 2026 announcement, underscoring the market's speculative volatility on global flashpoints.

question: Ink FDV above $500M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Ink's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Ink (http://inkonchain.com/) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will Mitch McConnell step down from the Senate before his term ends?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Mitch McConnell formally announces an intention to step down or otherwise vacates his U.S. Senate seat before his current term is scheduled to end (January 3, 2027, 11:59 PM ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

An announcement will qualify only if it indicates that McConnell will leave office prior to the scheduled end of his term. Statements reaffirming an existing plan to retire at the end of his term will not qualify.

The primary resolution sources will be official statements from Mitch McConnell, his office or representatives, and/or official communications from the U.S. Senate or government.

events.title: Will Mitch McConnell step down from the Senate before his term ends?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Mitch McConnell formally announces an intention to step down or otherwise vacates his U.S. Senate seat before his current term is scheduled to end (January 3, 2027, 11:59 PM ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

An announcement will qualify only if it indicates that McConnell will leave office prior to the scheduled end of his term. Statements reaffirming an existing plan to retire at the end of his term will not qualify.

The primary resolution sources will be official statements from Mitch McConnell, his office or representatives, and/or official communications from the U.S. Senate or government.

eventMetadata.context_description: Senate Minority Leader Mitch McConnell's February 28 announcement that he will step down from GOP leadership at year's end but serve his full Senate term until January 2027 forms the core driver of the 79.5% "No" odds on early resignation. Recent health setbacks, including public freezes in 2023 and a March 2024 concussion from which he has recovered and resumed duties, have fueled speculation, yet no official signals of retirement have emerged. McConnell's history of tenacity amid partisan pressures and internal GOP tensions, including clashes with former President Trump, reinforces trader confidence in his commitment to the seat through the 2026 cycle. Markets price in minimal near-term catalysts for change.

question: Will the chopsticks catch SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12 Superheavy booster?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the booster for the twelfth Starship-SuperHeavy test is successfully caught by the chopsticks on the Orbital Launch Tower during its landing attempt. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A successful catch is defined as the chopsticks capturing and supporting the booster without it falling to the ground or causing catastrophic damage during the landing process.

This market pertains to the twelfth Starship launch. If the rocket lifts off the pad, it will be considered the twelfth Starship launch, and this market will resolve based on it. If the rocket does not lift off the pad, it will not be considered the twelfth launch, and this market will stay open until the twelfth launch has occurred. If the twelfth launch has not occurred by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official video provided by SpaceX (https://www.youtube.com/c/SpaceX), as well as secondary video feeds and/or written reports if necessary.

events.title: SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12
events.description: This is a market on the outcome of the SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Starship Flight 6's aborted booster catch on November 19, 2024—due to a erroneous range safety hold-off—has sparked an FAA mishap investigation, dampening trader sentiment for Flight Test 12 by underscoring persistent reliability hurdles and regulatory delays that plague the program's cadence. SpaceX's rapid prep of Booster 14 and Ship 35 for Flight 7, targeting early 2025, aims for 25+ launches next year per Elon Musk's updates, but historical slips from initial timelines temper market-implied odds. Key risks include heat shield durability and catch mechanism refinements, amid competitive pressure from Blue Origin's New Glenn debut and NASA's Artemis reliance on Starship, with FAA approvals as the next major catalyst.

question: Will SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12 Superheavy explode?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the booster for the twelfth Starship-SuperHeavy test explodes at any point during the test from the start of fueling operations to 60 minutes after it makes contact with Earth upon landing. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An explosion is defined as a violent and catastrophic event resulting in the destruction of all or part of the vehicle, regardless of intent or context (e.g., a planned termination event would also count).

If the twelfth launch has not occurred by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

Note: The sixth Starship-SuperHeavy test flight which exploded after executing controlled splashdown in the Gulf of Mexico would qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official video provided by SpaceX (https://www.youtube.com/c/SpaceX), as well as secondary video feeds and/or written reports if necessary.

question: Will the Starship for SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12 achieve a successful splashdown?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Starship upper stage for the twelfth Starship-SuperHeavy test flight achieves a controlled splashdown in the ocean. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market pertains to the twelfth Starship launch. If the rocket lifts off the pad, it will be considered the twelfth Starship launch, and this market will resolve based on it. If the rocket does not lift off the pad, it will not be considered the twelfth launch, and this market will stay open until the twelfth launch has occurred. If the twelfth launch has not occurred by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

A splashdown will be considered controlled if the Starship upper stage successfully re-enters Earth's atmosphere, remains intact during descent, and enters the water without breaking apart or exploding upon impact.

The resolution source for this market will be official video provided by SpaceX (https://www.youtube.com/c/SpaceX), as well as secondary video feeds and/or written reports if necessary.

question: Will the Atlanta Hawks make the NBA Playoffs?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed team makes the 2025-26 NBA Playoffs. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Note: reaching the Play-In Tournament will not qualify as making the Playoffs. To make the NBA Playoffs, a team must earn one of the 16 spots in the postseason bracket.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to make the Playoffs based on the rules of the NBA (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated from reaching the Play-In), this market will resolve immediately to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NBA.

eventMetadata.context_description: With fewer than five games remaining in the 2023-24 regular season, the NBA playoff picture is crystalizing: Boston, New York, Milwaukee, Cleveland, Indiana, and Philadelphia have clinched East top-six spots, while Oklahoma City, Denver, Minnesota, the Clippers, and Mavericks hold West advantages. Trader consensus hinges on the chaotic Western play-in battle, where Phoenix, New Orleans, the Lakers (riding a four-game win streak), Warriors (slumping with three straight losses), and Kings vie for seeds 6-10 within three games of each other. Key factors include remaining schedules—Lakers face softer foes, Warriors the gauntlet—plus injuries like Zion Williamson's status and tiebreakers via head-to-head records, underscoring high upset potential in this compressed race.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed team makes the 2025-26 NBA Playoffs. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Note: reaching the Play-In Tournament will not qualify as making the Playoffs. To make the NBA Playoffs, a team must earn one of the 16 spots in the postseason bracket.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to make the Playoffs based on the rules of the NBA (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated from reaching the Play-In), this market will resolve immediately to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NBA.

eventMetadata.context_description: With fewer than five games remaining in the 2023-24 regular season, the NBA playoff picture is crystalizing: Boston, New York, Milwaukee, Cleveland, Indiana, and Philadelphia have clinched East top-six spots, while Oklahoma City, Denver, Minnesota, the Clippers, and Mavericks hold West advantages. Trader consensus hinges on the chaotic Western play-in battle, where Phoenix, New Orleans, the Lakers (riding a four-game win streak), Warriors (slumping with three straight losses), and Kings vie for seeds 6-10 within three games of each other. Key factors include remaining schedules—Lakers face softer foes, Warriors the gauntlet—plus injuries like Zion Williamson's status and tiebreakers via head-to-head records, underscoring high upset potential in this compressed race.

eventMetadata.context_description: With fewer than five games remaining in the 2023-24 regular season, the NBA playoff picture is crystalizing: Boston, New York, Milwaukee, Cleveland, Indiana, and Philadelphia have clinched East top-six spots, while Oklahoma City, Denver, Minnesota, the Clippers, and Mavericks hold West advantages. eventMetadata.context_description: Orlando Magic hold a slim edge over Atlanta Hawks in the Southeast Division standings at 19-12 versus 18-13, fueling the razor-thin trader consensus of 31.0% and 30.9% implied probabilities, as both teams boast top-10 offenses but middling defenses. question: Will the Boston Celtics finish with the best record in the NBA?
description:
This market will resolve according to the team that finishes the 2025-2026 NBA Regular Season with the best record in the league.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to finish the regular Season with the best record.

If multiple teams are tied for most wins in the 2025-26 NBA Regular Season, this market will resolve in favor of the team who receives home court advantage for the 2026 NBA playoffs. A list of the NBA’s Tiebreak Procedures can be found here: https://ak-static-int.nba.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2017/06/NBA_Tiebreaker_Procedures.pdf

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus pins the Indiana Pacers as frontrunners for the NBA's worst record at 51.6%, fueled by their 2-6 start marred by Tyrese Haliburton's hamstring injury and a brutal schedule facing top Western Conference teams, eroding early-season momentum. The Washington Wizards trail at 33% with the league's worst 1-8 mark, embracing a rebuild sans Kyle Kuzma's full impact amid defensive woes. Sacramento Kings (10.5%) slip due to De'Aaron Fox's absence and road-heavy slate, while Brooklyn Nets (5.9%) and Memphis Grizzlies (4.3%) linger on tanking narratives, youth inexperience, and Ja Morant's questionable status. Recent losses and official injury reports amplify uncertainty in this volatile race.

question: Will Victor Wembanyama win the 2025–2026 NBA Defensive Player of the Year?
description:
This market will resolve according to the player who is awarded the 2025–26 NBA Defensive Player of the Year.

If the listed player is not announced as a finalist for the 2025–26 Defensive Player of the Year, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NBA. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: NBA Defensive Player of the Year Winner

eventMetadata.context_description: Victor Wembanyama's league-leading 3.6 blocks per game, plus top rankings in defensive win shares and box-plus-minus, anchor the Spurs' leap from 27th to 9th in defensive rating, fueling his 87.9% implied probability as traders price in his historic rim protection and versatility. Chet Holmgren trails at 8% amid Oklahoma City's No. 1 defense, but lacks Wembanyama's individual dominance in steals and blocks. Recent consistency—Wembanyama's 30+ games with 5+ combined steals/blocks—solidifies the gap, though injury absences or a Holmgren surge in OKC's playoff push could narrow it, as voters often reward team success in close races. Upsets remain possible given DPOY's subjective blend of stats and narrative.
